title: 13 Currency Settlement, Charges, Reports, and Trace Requests
body_text:
13	Currency Settlement, Charges, Reports, and Trace Requests
13.1	Currency Order Settlement
Currency orders are settled on the system advised dispatch date. Charges are debited to either the branch receiving the currency order or the branch responsible for the offsite ATM. Charges are posted to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs current account at Central 1.
Canadian Currency
Canadian currency orders are posted to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs Canadian dollar current account using transaction code CP.
US Currency
US currency orders are posted to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs US current account using transaction code CP.
If your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs current account was not debited for Canadian and/or US currency orders, contact Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Client Support Services. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
13.2	Currency Return Settlement
Your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs current account will be credited once Royal Bank processes the deposit and settles with Central 1. Royal Bank generally settles currency returns on the date they are received. If your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs current account (Canadian and/or US) isnГўВҖВҷt credited for currency returns, contact Paper Payments (
tracers1@central1.com
).
The deposit slip isnГўВҖВҷt returned to your financial institution as confirmation of receipt.
13.3	Currency Order Charges
Service charges are debited in Canadian funds at month-end to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs settlement transit number. Charges for US currency orders are amalgamated with those from other orders placed before the Central 1 order deadline. For example, if your financial institution has multiple currency orders for different locations, including some in US funds, service charges will be posted by settlement transit to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs Canadian dollar current account, using transaction code BF.
Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
13.4	Currency Return Charges
Financial institutions are not charged for printing or delivery of Canadian or US dollar deposit slips.
Royal BankГўВҖВҷs charges are based on the number of bank notes or boxes returned. At the end of the month, Central 1 posts one charge to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs Central 1 account. Since the charges are not broken down by deposit, make sure you keep deposit slips throughout the month to facilitate balancing.
Additionally, there is a deposit processing fee charged per deposit slip. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
13.5	Cash Services Settlement Reports
Central 1 provides daily Account Activity and Balances reports for currency transaction reporting:
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the AIBC (for BC financial institutions) and on the AIOC (for Ontario financial institutions) with the transaction code CP.
US dollar transactions are reported on the AIBU (for BC financial institutions) and on the AIOU (for Ontario financial institutions) with the transaction code CP.
In addition, Central 1 provides the following daily and monthly settlement reports for currency orders:
CSDSMMDD.csv is the daily report, available Monday to Friday (business days).
CSMSMMDD.csv is the monthly report, available on the first business day of each month.
You can download these reports from Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange. If applicable, PDFs of these reports are available in the Banking folder. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information.
Postings can also be viewed on Current Account Services (CAS) Online. See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for more information.
13.6	Currency Trace Requests
You can submit currency trace requests for the following currency inquiries:
Cash Parcel Not Received at a Branch Location
ГўВҖВ“ If a currency order isnГўВҖВҷt received at the specified location, contact your armoured car service. Provide them with the parcel number (i.e., the Cash Services order confirmation number) and the following, as applicable:
ГўВҲВ’
If the missing order contains notes only (CAD and/or US) or coins only, add the number ГўВҖВң1ГўВҖВқ to the end of the confirmation number (e.g., 1551390-1).
ГўВҲВ’
If the missing order contains notes (CAD and/or US) and coins, add the number ГўВҖВң2ГўВҖВқ to the end of the confirmation number (e.g., 1551415-2).
Note:	If your armoured car service canГўВҖВҷt locate the missing order, contact Central 1ГўВҖВҷs AFT Desk. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Discrepancies in Adjustments for Returned Cash Parcels
ГўВҖВ“ If your financial institution disagrees with the Royal Bank adjustment for a returned cash parcel, see Section 11.12, Discrepancies in Returned Cash Parcels.
Discrepancies in Incoming Cash Parcels
ГўВҖВ“ If the value of an incoming cash parcel from Royal Bank does not correspond to the value ordered, see Section 10.5, Handling Discrepancies in Incoming Cash Parcels.
END
